Home/Amalfi Coast/Praiano

Amalfi Coast · Sunset & Quiet

Praiano

Between Positano and Amalfi, Praiano is the village the crowds pass through without stopping — which is exactly what makes it one of the best-kept secrets on the coast. The best sunset view in the entire Amalfi Coast is here, from the terrace of San Gennaro.

Praiano is split into two hamlets: Vettica Maggiore (the upper part, along the main road) and Marina di Praia (the small bay at sea level, reachable via steps or by car through a tunnel). The village is known for its diving conditions, its August candle festival (the Luminaria di San Domenico) and for having the best west-facing sunset view on the coast.

Did You Know?

2,000

residents — fewer than anywhere else on the coast

1–4

August: the Luminaria di San Domenico candle festival

270°

sea view from the San Gennaro terrace

What to See and Do

San Gennaro Sunset Terrace

The west-facing terrace of the Church of San Gennaro in Vettica Maggiore is the finest sunset viewpoint on the Amalfi Coast, with an unobstructed view toward Capri and the open sea. Free, open at all hours.

Marina di Praia

A tiny bay enclosed between two cliffs, with a pebble beach, fishing boats and a few beach bars. Reachable by steps from the SS163 or through a rock tunnel by car. One of the most photogenic spots on the coast.

Gavitella Beach

A cove with clear water accessible by boat or via a long staircase. Quieter than Marina di Praia; ideal for snorkelling.

Luminaria di San Domenico (1–4 August)

Thousands of candles are lit every evening to honour the Dominican tradition. On 4 August the finale includes fire performances and projections. Free access.

How to Get Here

SITA Bus ⭐

From Positano or Amalfi

The SS163 SITA bus stops in Praiano. About 20 min from Positano, 25 min from Amalfi. ~€1.30.

By Car

SS163 Amalfitana

Limited paid parking near the main road. In summer, traffic on the SS163 can be very slow.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do you get to Praiano from Positano?
By SITA bus from Positano: about 20 minutes, ~€1.30. The bus stops along the SS163 main road. From there you walk down steps to the various beaches and hamlets.
What is the best sunset viewpoint in Praiano?
The terrace of the Church of San Gennaro in the hamlet of Vettica Maggiore offers one of the best sunsets on the entire Amalfi Coast, with a view extending to Capri on the horizon.
Is Praiano less crowded than Positano?
Yes, significantly. Praiano is one of the quieter villages on the Amalfi Coast. It receives far fewer day-trippers than Positano or Amalfi and retains a more authentic, local character.

Fast Facts — Praiano

Location
Amalfi Coast, SA
Population
~2,000 inhabitants
From Positano
~20 min (SITA bus)
From Amalfi
~25 min (SITA bus)
Beaches
Marina di Praia, Gavitella
Best for
Sunset, quiet, diving
Best time
May–Oct
